data.title: Intel Tests Chipmaking Tools Linked to China, Sparks Security Fears
data.description:
Intel (INTC) has reportedly tested chipmaking equipment this year from ACM Research (ACMR), a Fremont, California-based supplier, according to two sources cited by Reuters. Two ACM units, based in Shanghai and South Korea, were added to the U.S. Entity List in 2024 over allegations of aiding China's military use of commercial technology and advanced chip production. However, ACM denies the claims.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Potential Risks from Intel's Supply Choices

Intel previously faced calls for CEO Lip-Bu Tan's resignation over alleged China ties from President Donald Trump in August. Intel tested two wet-etch tools, used for removing material from the silicon wafers that are transformed into semiconductors, for possible use in its most advanced chipmaking process, known as 14A. That process is due for an initial launch in 2027.

ACM declined to comment on specific customers but confirmed its U.S. team sold and delivered multiple tools from Asian operations to U.S. clients. ACM also disclosed shipping three tools to a major U.S. semiconductor maker, which are being tested and some of which have met performance standards.

Intel's Tests Raise Security Concerns

The tests have drawn scrutiny from China hawks, especially now that Intel is partly owned by the U.S. government. Critics warn of tech leaks to China, replacing trusted suppliers, and sabotage risks as Chinese toolmakers expand globally. Bipartisan lawmakers recently reintroduced a bill to bar U.S.-subsidized chipmakers from using Chinese equipment in expansions.

In defense, ACM stated that it poses no national security risk and emphasized its U.S. operations are "bifurcated and isolated" from the sanctioned Shanghai unit. U.S. customers receive direct support from American staff with strong protections for trade secrets.

Is INTC Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analysts remain cautious about Intel's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, INTC stock has a Hold consensus rating based on five Buys, 24 Holds, and six Sell ratings. The average Intel price target of $37.04 implies 6.3% downside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, INTC shares have surged nearly 97%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Rocket Lab Stock Forecast — What's Next for RKLB After Its Big Surge?
data.description:
Shares of Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) are gaining investor attention lately, surging on the back of strong operational updates and growing market interest in small‑satellite launches. The stock's recent rally has prompted questions about what lies ahead for the company and whether RKLB's momentum can continue. RKLB shares jumped more than 10% on Thursday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For context, Rocket Lab is a space launch company specializing in small orbital rockets and satellite solutions, best known for its Electron rocket and upcoming Neutron launch vehicle.

What's Driving the Recent Rally?

Over the last five days, RKLB stock has gained roughly 30%.

Earlier this week, RKLB's surge was fueled by excitement in the satellite sector, following reports that Elon Musk's SpaceX (PC:SPXEX) is targeting a $1.5 trillion IPO in 2026. SpaceX's huge valuation could potentially benefit Rocket Lab in the short term. The influx of attention and capital could also create new opportunities for RKLB, including partnerships, niche launch services, and specialized satellite deployments.

Additionally, Rocket Lab completed final qualification tests on the "Hungry Hippo" cargo fairing for its next-generation, partially reusable Neutron rocket, expected to launch early next year. Despite a minor launch delay, investors responded with aggressive buying, driving the stock higher.

High Hopes on Neutron Launch

Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket is a next-generation, partially reusable launch vehicle designed to carry medium-to-large satellites into orbit. It aims to compete in the growing commercial space sector, offering cost-effective and flexible launch solutions.

Looking ahead, analysts remain optimistic about the company's upcoming Neutron rocket launch, scheduled for 2026. Once operational, it could compete with SpaceX's Falcon 9 by offering a smaller, lower-cost payload option while maintaining similar reusability features. Recently, Rocket Lab VP Shaun D'Mello said, "A rocket like Neutron has never been built before, and we're doing it at a pace and price point that will bring the innovation and competition the industry needs today."

On Wall Street, Baird's five-star-rated analyst Peter Arment has set the highest price target for RKLB at $83, implying more than 30% upside from current levels. He believes Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket could make the company a direct competitor to SpaceX's Falcon 9, aiming for the "highly lucrative medium-lift market."

Is RKLB a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, RKLB stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with nine Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Rocket Lab is $65.17, suggesting an upside of 2.6% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/12/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Investors Move Out of AI Plays
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed early Friday, after investors shifted from artificial intelligence (AI) stocks to other categories in the broader market following Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal second-quarter results. The database software and cloud computing company missed revenue expectations and sparked concerns about elevated AI spending. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) were down 0.21%, while futures tied to the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.03% and 0.29%, respectively, at 4:14 a.m. EST on December 12.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

During Thursday's regular trading session, the Dow Jones and the S&P 500 rose 1.34% and 0.21%, respectively, reaching record highs. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.26% as investors moved away from high-flying technology stocks.

Meanwhile, shares of chip company Broadcom (AVGO) were down 4.1% in Friday's pre-market trading despite reporting better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2025.

The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.168%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.05 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,312 per ounce.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as record sessions for two major U.S. indexes boosted investor sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Higher

Asia-Pacific markets closed higher following the new records hit by the Dow Jones and the S&P 500, as well as the recent 25 basis points interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.75% higher. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.41%, while the Shenzhen Component was up 0.84%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 1.37%, and the Topix rose 1.98%.

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Scores Partial Victory in Epic Games Antitrust Lawsuit
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) secured a partial win on Thursday after a U.S. appeals court agreed to reverse parts of an earlier order that would have forced major changes to its highly profitable App Store. However, the court also upheld a broader injunction from the Epic Games (PC:EPCGM) antitrust case.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For context, the legal battle between Apple and Epic Games began in August 2020, when Epic attempted to bypass Apple's 30% App Store commission for in-app purchases of Fortnite. Apple responded by removing Fortnite from the App Store, prompting Epic to sue, alleging anti-competitive practices. The case went to trial in 2021 and has continued through appeals.

While Apple largely won, a 2021 court order required it to let developers include links to alternative payment options. Apple partially complied but introduced a 27% commission on purchases made outside the App Store within seven days of using a link, in addition to its standard 30% App Store fee. Epic argued this violated the previous court order and asked the court to hold Apple in contempt, while Apple denied breaking any rules.

Mixed Outcome for Apple

Apple went to an appeals court to change a previous court order that forced it to make changes to the App Store. The court agreed to modify some parts of the order, saying they were too broad. Notably, the judges agreed to overturn the part of the order that prevented Apple from charging commissions on payments made outside the App Store.

However, most of the original ruling still stands, including the main restrictions and penalties from the earlier court decision in the lawsuit filed by Fortnite maker Epic Games. In short, Apple got a partial win, but not everything it wanted.

Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney praised the latest ruling, saying it prevents Apple from imposing huge extra fees on developers and will help both developers and consumers. He added that, after years of Apple blocking changes, this decision will finally bring major improvements.

Apple's Risk Analysis

According to TipRanks' Risk Analysis Tool, Apple's Legal & Regulatory Risks account for 18.5% of the overall risks, above the sector average of 17.3%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Top Bank of America Analyst Downgrades PayPal Stock (PYPL), Says 'Risk Reward Is Balanced'
data.description:
PayPal (PYPL) stock has declined 8.5% over the past month and is down 28% year-to-date, as the fintech company disappointed investors with a Q3 revenue miss and softer guidance. Top Bank of America analyst Mihir Bhatia downgraded PayPal stock from Buy to Hold and slashed the price target to $68 from $93, saying "risk-reward is balanced."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

In contrast, Bhatia upgraded Visa (V) stock to Buy from Hold, noting solid upside potential and stablecoin prospects.

Top Analyst Moves to the Sidelines on PayPal Stock

Bhatia noted that PayPal's efforts to revive growth in its core branded checkout are taking longer than expected and are limiting near-term upside potential. The analyst had expected product innovation and the upgraded checkout solution to boost PayPal usage at checkout. However, Q4 is expected to see a slowdown in branded checkout growth. Moreover, 2026 will be an investment year, as the company tries to boost its business.

While Bhatia views PayPal's base of 400 million consumers and merchant accounts, as well as the faster pace of innovation under new management, favorably, he thinks that the stock's risk-reward looks balanced. He prefers to stay on the sidelines until there is more clarity on the company's turnaround efforts achieving the desired results.

Bhatia lowered his earnings estimates for 2026 and 2027 by 2% and 4%, respectively, to reflect slower growth and higher investments.

Is PYPL Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Given the ongoing challenges, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on PayPal Holdings stock based on 19 Holds, nine Buys, and three Sell recommendations. The average PYPL stock price target of $79.23 indicates 28.4% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: TLRY, ACB, CGC: Why Are Cannabis Stocks Up Today?
data.description:
Shares of cannabis stocks, Tilray Brands (TLRY), Aurora Cannabis (ACB), and Canopy Growth (CGC), jumped more than 30% in after-hours trading on Thursday after reports indicated that the U.S. government is preparing to significantly ease federal restrictions on marijuana.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

In pre-market trading, CGC stock is up 35.4%, TLRY has gained 31.5%, and ACB is up 30.54%.

Trump Pushes Plan to Loosen Federal Marijuana Rules

On Thursday, a report by The Washington Post said President Donald Trump is expected to direct federal agencies to reclassify marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III. Schedule I substances like heroin and LSD are considered to have no accepted medical use and a high potential for abuse, while Schedule III drugs have recognized medical uses and a lower risk of abuse.

Trump reportedly spoke with House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-Louisiana) during a Wednesday call from the Oval Office to discuss the plan. According to sources, the president is preparing an executive order that would instruct federal agencies to move forward with reclassifying marijuana, making it easier to access.

However, a White House spokesperson told Forbes that no final decisions have been made regarding marijuana rescheduling.

What It Means for Cannabis Stocks

A potential move to reclassify marijuana to Schedule III would be a major turning point for U.S. cannabis companies. It would ease federal restrictions, reduce tax burdens, and make it easier for businesses to access banking, financing, and insurance. Additionally, the shift would reduce regulatory hurdles, open the door for broader medical and commercial use, and potentially draw more institutional investors into the sector.

For stocks like TLRY, CGC, and ACB, the news signals a path toward a more legitimate, less restricted operating environment —which is why shares reacted so sharply. If rescheduling goes forward, it could mark the beginning of a more stable, scalable future for the entire cannabis sector.

data.title: GOOGL vs. OpenAI: Analysts Weigh In on the Better AI Play
data.description:
The artificial intelligence (AI) race is accelerating as companies vie to capture a larger share of the potential trillion-dollar market. Alphabet (GOOGL) and OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) represent two powerhouse players in the AI landscape. Alphabet, with its vast ecosystem, trades publicly, while OpenAI, valued at over $150 billion privately, remains off exchanges for now. Yet, investors often pit GOOGL's strong ecosystem against OpenAI's innovation, weighing GOOGL shares against indirect exposure via partners like Microsoft (MSFT).

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Google integrates its Gemini 2.0 models (launched in 2025) across search, cloud, and custom TPUs, drawing on billions of users for data superiority. OpenAI, on the other hand, focuses on frontier models like its GPT series and ChatGPT subscriptions, prioritizing research and ethics.

Let's examine analysts' views on the AI race leader.

Jefferies Remains Bullish on OpenAI's Momentum

Jefferies is bullish on OpenAI's newly-launched GPT-5.2 model, which exceeded expectations by surpassing GPT-5.1 and narrowing the gap with Google's Gemini. The research firm noted that strong performance in enterprise tasks and coding, plus a top-tier CRO hire, bolster confidence in OpenAI's FY26 enterprise strategy. The firm highlights rapid AI model improvements driving faster adoption than past tech shifts, creating upside for Microsoft (MSFT), which is a 27% OpenAI stakeholder, Oracle (ORCL), and CoreWeave (CRWV) due to their deep ties.

Importantly, OpenAI is reportedly planning for an initial public offering (IPO) in late 2026 or 2027 and has shifted to a for-profit structure. Google faces a real challenge once OpenAI lists shares, broadening retail access.

Evercore: Google Search Solidifies AI Lead

Evercore ISI analyst Mark Mahaney reiterated his Buy rating on GOOGL stock with a $325 price target, implying 4% upside potential. Despite OpenAI's progress, he noted that Google's core search business share climbed to 76% in November from 70% in August, per their survey, with no drop in commercial queries. He noted that Google's AI integrations improved the overall search experience, boosting usage volumes and signaling strong revenue potential. Notably, Google's Gemini 3.0 model, integrated into search in November, recently outperformed rivals like GPT-5.

GOOGL shares are up over 65% in 2025, fueled by AI momentum and custom chip development. Benchmarks show Gemini edging out competitors in key areas, though OpenAI's innovations keep pressure on.

Which Is the Best Listed AI Player?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which is the best listed AI player. Currently, Microsoft, Meta (META), GOOGL, Micron (MU), Amazon (AMZN), and Nvidia (NVDA) have earned analysts' Strong Buy consensus rating, with NVDA stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Hit by Tech Weakness as Jobless Claims Normalize
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) closed Thursday's trading session higher, while the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) ended with a loss. Both ETFs were affected by weakness in the tech and AI sectors after Oracle (ORCL) reported disappointing earnings results, with major players like Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) sustaining losses in sympathy.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Before the market open, the Department of Labor announced that initial jobless claims for the week ended December 6 surged by 44,000 to 236,000, registering the largest weekly rise since 2020 and topping the consensus estimate of 220,000. At the same time, initial claims data for the previous week, which came in at a three-year low, were likely skewed because they included the Thanksgiving holiday.

"Don't read too much into the jump in jobless claims," said Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long. "Smoothing it out, this still looks like an economy averaging 215,000 to 220,000 new jobless claims a week. That's not a cause for concern." Continuing jobless claims, which track ongoing unemployment benefit recipients, were 1.838 million for the week ended November 22, below the estimate of 1.95 million.

Meanwhile, the U.S. trade deficit improved to $52.8 billion in September, marking its lowest level in over five years and below the estimate of $63.1 billion. However, the year-to-date deficit is still 17.2%, or $112.6 billion, higher than the same period in 2024. During the month, exports of non-monetary gold and consumer goods rose, while imports of computers, computer accessories, and electronics fell. Notably, the U.S. trade deficit with China fell by $4 billion to $11.4 billion, while its deficit with Switzerland during the previous month changed to a surplus of $6.6 billion. The deficit is calculated by subtracting exports from imports.

The falling deficit comes despite November's tariff revenue falling to $30.76 billion from $31.35 billion in October. President Trump has toned down his aggressive tariff stance in recent weeks and signed an executive order last month that excludes certain food and beverage items from reciprocal tariffs, including coffee, tomatoes, oranges, and beef. Trump also told Politico that he is considering additional tariff exclusions on consumer staple products.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.21% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.35%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Broadcom Stock Earnings, Anthropic Deal, Analyst Ratings – Here's All You Need to Know!
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) reported blockbuster Q4 fiscal results yesterday. Shares initially rallied but fell 4.5% in after-hours trading as investors fretted over delayed OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) revenue. Interestingly, CEO Hock Tan revealed that its secret $10 billion customer was Anthropic (PC:ANTPQ), and the company just signed an additional $11 billion deal with the AI startup.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.95 surpassed analysts' consensus estimate of $1.87. Sales also surged 28.2% year-over-year to $18.02 billion, topping expectations of $17.47 billion. Moreover, Broadcom secured a $1 billion order from an undisclosed fifth AI chip client. Tan also noted a $73 billion "minimum" AI order backlog, to ship over the next six quarters.

Analysts Raise AVGO Price Targets

Despite investor concerns, analysts praised Broadcom's AI progress via custom ASIC chips.

Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh maintained his Buy rating, while raising the price target from $435 to $450, implying 10.7% upside potential. Rakesh was impressed by AVGO's better-than-expected Q1 FY26 guidance of $19.1 billion in sales. He forecasts full-year 2026 revenue of about $45 billion, driven by a $73 billion 18-month backlog. He also noted that Anthropic's orders doubled to $21 billion, along with a new fifth customer contributing $1 billion, and OpenAI likely becoming the sixth customer in FY27–FY29 with a 10-gigawatt deal.

Similarly, TD Cowen analyst Joshua Buchalter kept his Buy rating and lifted the price target from $405 to $450. He noted that investors had high hopes for the earnings report, expecting boosts from AI businesses like Ethernet switching and TPUs. The actual results and guidance met those hopes, with Q1 AI revenue hitting $8.2 billion and beating Buchalter's model by $1 billion. The $73 billion AI backlog (over 18 months) could have disappointed. He added that investors expected the OpenAI deal to start soon and add significantly, but it likely is not included in the backlog yet. Moreover, a new fifth customer ordered $1 billion for late FY26 delivery, but investors thought this was OpenAI, and $1 billion is too small for that.

Is AVGO Stock a Buy?

On TipRanks, AVGO stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 24 Buys and one Hold rating. The average Broadcom price target of $436.33 implies 7.4% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, AVGO stock has surged more than 76%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
